task_completion.description: Import the scanned UAV wing STL file into OpenVSP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP workspace or Geometry Browser shows an imported mesh (typically named 'MeshGeom' or similar), and the 3D viewport displays the scanned wing mesh.

task_completion.description: Add a new standard Wing component to the model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Geometry Browser lists a new 'WingGeom' component alongside the imported mesh, and a default wing appears in the 3D viewport.

task_completion.description: Adjust the Wing component's Span, Root Chord, and Tip Chord to match the imported mesh.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WingGeom parameter window (Plan tab) shows Span ~10.0, Root Chord ~2.0, and Tip Chord ~1.0, OR the 3D viewport shows the new wing's width and chord lengths closely matching the underlying mesh.

task_completion.description: Adjust the Wing component's Sweep and Dihedral to match the imported mesh.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WingGeom parameter window shows Sweep ~5.0 degrees and Dihedral ~3.0 degrees, OR the 3D viewport shows the new wing angled backward and upward to perfectly align with the mesh.

task_completion.description: Ensure the 3D outline of the new Wing component closely overlaps and aligns with the imported STL mesh.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 3D viewport, the parametric WingGeom and the imported MeshGeom are visually superimposed, showing a tight geometric match in shape, size, and orientation.

task_completion.description: Save the reconstructed model to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or the OpenVSP main window title bar shows the file being saved as 'reconstructed_wing.vsp3' in the '/home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenVSP graphical user interface to perform the reverse engineering.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenVSP application open with the agent clicking through menus (File > Import, Geom > Wing) and adjusting sliders/values in the parameter windows.

integrity.description: The final model contains both the original STL mesh and the new Wing component.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP Geometry Browser explicitly lists both the 'MeshGeom' (or imported STL) and the 'WingGeom' at the time of saving.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by manually writing or editing the .vsp3 XML file in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no screenshots of the agent opening 'reconstructed_wing.vsp3' in nano, vim, gedit, or VSCode to manually type in the expected parameters.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The exact target parameters for the wing are: Span = 10.0, Root Chord = 2.0, Tip Chord = 1.0, Sweep = 5.0 degrees, and Dihedral = 3.0 degrees. A 10% tolerance is acceptable for all values. The final saved file must be named 'reconstructed_wing.vsp3' and must contain both the original mesh and the newly created wing geometry.
